title: ranunculus flabellatus var. nidulans
intro: Ranunculus flabellatus var. nidulans, also known as the alpine buttercup, is a plant species in the Ranunculaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, acne, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Ranunculus flabellatus var. nidulans typically involve infusions or tinctures made from the plant's roots and leaves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its benefits.
